description: Lock the 2026-06-27 border_customs_posts correction to Roman portorium stationes as customs checkpoints and tax offices, not generic road toll funding or archived customs records.

checks.reason: The source-backed reliable chronology is the second century BCE for Roman portorium evidence, not the old Classical era placeholder.
Monetary valuation enables ad valorem customs duties without making coinage the only possible tariff pathway.
Stationes checked merchandise and collected transport/customs taxes, so tax calculation and collection are the direct administrative capability.
Public archives are broader than the scoped checkpoint and tax-office mechanism.
The node remains customs/tariff administration, not Roman road-maintenance toll funding.
